description: coder-workflow hooks — safety guards, graph maintenance, task tracking, intelligence features (experience journal, quality guardian, bug hunter, consistency enforcer, tech debt tracker, preflight), telemetry.

hooks.command: bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); PROMPT=$(jq -r ".prompt // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 140); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] PROMPT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$PROMPT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; exit 0'
bash

bash -c 'DB=.codegraph/graph.db; LOCK=.codegraph/scan.lock; if [ ! -f "$DB" ]; then touch "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null || true; (command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow scan 2>/dev/null || true); rm -f "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; exit 0'

bash -c '
# Plugin conflict detection: warn if both global + project-local installs exist
GLOBAL="$HOME/.claude/skills/coder-workflow/skills"
LOCAL=".claude/skills/coder-workflow"
if [ -d "$GLOBAL" ] && [ -d "$LOCAL" ]; then
  echo "coder-workflow: Dual install detected. Project-local (.claude/) takes precedence."
  echo "  Remove one to avoid conflicts:"
  echo "    rm -rf ~/.claude/skills/coder-workflow  # global"
  echo "    rm -rf .claude/skills/coder-workflow     # project-local"
fi
exit 0'
bash -c 'echo "🧠 BELAJAR: Ada pengalaman dari sesi lalu. /experience recent" 2>/dev/null; exit 0'
bash
echo 'Context was compacted and then resumed. Skills, orchestrator, and CodeGraph MCP tools are still active. Re-read your active task before proceeding.'
echo 'Session cleared. Clean slate — use /coder-workflow:coder-orchestrator for any coding task.'
bash -c 'mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null; echo 0 > .claude/stop-block.count; exit 0'
bash
bash

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+reset\s+--hard|git\s+clean\s+-[a-zA-Z]*f"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: git reset --hard or git clean -f will destroy uncommitted work. Ensure nothing unsaved exists before proceeding.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "DROP\s+(TABLE|DATABASE|SCHEMA)|TRUNCATE\s+TABLE"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: Destructive SQL detected (DROP/TRUNCATE). Confirm this is targeting a dev/test database, not production.\"}" ; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+merge\s+"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: git merge detected. If this involves parallel worktree changes, verify no conflicts before completing. Run git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U after merge to check.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+commit"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: You are about to commit. Consider invoking the \\\"todo-checker\\\" subagent to ensure no dummy code exists, or \\\"/docs\\\" to update documentation.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if printf "%s" "$CMD" | grep -qE "git\s+push"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: You are about to push to remote. Run \\\"/review\\\" to automatically audit your code for security flaws before pushing.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow OVERPOWER DIRECTIVE: Before writing, verify that you are NOT writing dummy code, mock structures, or simple band-aids. Over-engineer this! Drill down to the root cause. If you output a lazy shortcut or suppress warnings, you fail.\"}"; exit 0'
bash

bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: You are in an Explore context. You should prioritize mcp__codegraph__query_graph, mcp__codegraph__search_code, mcp__codegraph__analyze_impact, or mcp__codegraph__summarize_architecture to explore the codebase. Fallback to bash find/grep gracefully if graph tools fail or timeout.\"}"'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); AGENT=$(jq -r ".tool_input.subagent_type // .tool_input.Subagents[0].TypeName // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$AGENT" | grep -qi "explore"; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow: The Explore agent should prioritize mcp__codegraph tools (query_graph, search_code, analyze_impact). Fallback to bash find/grep if graph tools fail.\"}"; fi; exit 0'

bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: "🧠 BELAJAR: Sebelum nulis code, cek experience journal.
⚡ RAJIN: Jangan tinggal TODO/FIXME.

/preflight untuk readiness check."}"'
bash -c 'jq -n "{systemMessage: \"⚡ RAJIN: Sebelum commit — /preflight, /bughunt, /debt scan. Zero dummy code!\"}"'

bash -c 'jq -n "{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: \"PostToolUse\", additionalContext: \"coder-workflow: Impact Radius active — bugs found within your modified files must be tracked as low-priority tasks to be fixed at the end of the session, preventing feature starvation. Ignore pre-existing debt outside your FILE_MANIFEST.\"}}"'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); LOG=.claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log; mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; [ -f "$LOG" ] && SIZE=$(stat -c%s "$LOG" 2>/dev/null || echo 0) && [ "$SIZE" -gt 10485760 ] && tail -n +500 "$LOG" > "$LOG.tmp" && mv "$LOG.tmp" "$LOG"; printf "[%s] WRITE: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$FILE" >> "$LOG" 2>/dev/null; exit 0'

bash -c '
# Debounce: allow update at most once every 5 seconds.
# Multiple rapid writes share one update — flock serializes + timestamp check.
DEBOUNCE_FILE=.codegraph/.update-debounce
LOCK_FILE=.codegraph/.update-debounce.lock
(
  flock -x 200
  NOW=$(date +%s)
  LAST=0
  if [ -f "$DEBOUNCE_FILE" ]; then
    LAST=$(cat "$DEBOUNCE_FILE" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 0)
  fi
  ELAPSED=$((NOW - LAST))
  if [ "$ELAPSED" -ge 5 ]; then
    mkdir -p .codegraph 2>/dev/null || true
    printf "%s" "$NOW" > "$DEBOUNCE_FILE"
    if test -f .codegraph/graph.db; then
      command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow update >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
  fi
) 200>"$LOCK_FILE"
exit 0'

bash -c '
# ─── Quality Guardian: snapshot after write ───
if command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  FILE=$(jq -r ".tool_input.file_path // .tool_input.path // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$FILE" ] && [ -f "$FILE" ]; then
    # Bug hunt scan on written file (async, debounced)
    coder-workflow bughunt scan-file --file "$FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    # Consistency check hint
    BASENAME=$(basename "$FILE")
    if echo "$BASENAME" | grep -qiE "\.(ts|js|tsx|jsx|py|go|rs|java|kt)$"; then
      coder-workflow consistency validate "$FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 &
    fi
  fi
fi
exit 0'
bash -c 'if test -f .codegraph/graph.db; then command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow update >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; exit 0'
hooks.if: Bash(git checkout *)|Bash(git switch *)|Bash(git merge *)|Bash(git pull)|Bash(git rebase *)

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TOOL=$(jq -r ".tool_name // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); ERR=$(jq -r ".tool_error // .error // empty" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] FAIL [%s]: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TOOL" "$ERR" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; if [ -n "$TOOL" ] && [ -n "$ERR" ]; then command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow learn-log --type tool_failure --tool "$TOOL" --error "$ERR" 2>/dev/null || true; fi; exit 0'

bash -c '
MAX_WAIT=10
WAITED=0
while [ $WAITED -lt $MAX_WAIT ]; do
  if [ -f .codegraph/.update-debounce.lock ]; then
    flock -n .codegraph/.update-debounce.lock -c "true" 2>/dev/null && break || sleep 0.5
  else break; fi
  WAITED=$((WAITED+1))
done
git add . 2>/dev/null
if git diff --cached --quiet; then
  echo "Turn complete. Tasks tracked, quality OK."
else
  MSG=$(git diff --cached | head -n 500 | claude -p "Write 1-line conventional commit message for these staged changes. Output ONLY raw message." --setting-sources user --no-session-persistence 2>/dev/null | tail -n 1)
  [ -z "$MSG" ] && MSG="chore: session auto-commit"
  git commit -m "$MSG" 2>/dev/null || true
  echo "Auto-committed."
fi
test -f .codegraph/graph.db && command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow update >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow experience record --type turn-end --outcome success 2>/dev/null || true
'
echo 'API error. Resume with /resume when ready.'
bash -c 'command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow learn-log --type stop_failure --error "session" 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
echo 'Package manifest changed on disk. Run the appropriate install command before using any new or updated packages.'
echo 'Env file changed on disk. Verify it is gitignored and contains no committed secrets before the next git operation.'
echo 'CLAUDE.md changed on disk — project instructions updated. New rules apply to subsequent turns immediately.'
echo 'hooks.json changed on disk — hook configuration updated. Changes take effect on the next hook event.'
echo 'TypeScript/linter config changed on disk. A rebuild may be needed: npm run build && npm run typecheck'
echo 'MCP configuration changed on disk. Restart Claude Code (or reload plugins) for the new server configuration to take effect.'
echo 'Agent definition changed on disk. New agent instructions apply to subsequent subagent spawns.'
echo 'Skill definition changed on disk. New skill rules apply immediately — invoke the skill to pick up updated behavior.'
echo 'Experience journal updated — knowledge from past sessions is available.'
echo 'Tech debt data changed on disk.'

bash -c 'AGENT=${1:-unknown}; LOCK=.claude/agent-depth.lock; mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null; printf "[%s] AGENT: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$AGENT" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; (flock -x 200; DEPTH=$(cat "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 0); NEW=$((DEPTH+1)); printf "%s" "$NEW" > "$LOCK"; if [ "$NEW" -gt 2 ]; then jq -n "{systemMessage: \"DEPTH LIMIT: Agent nesting > 2 max. Stop delegating.\"}"; fi) 200>.claude/agent-depth.lock.flock; exit 0'

bash -c 'LOCK=.claude/agent-depth.lock; (flock -x 200; DEPTH=$(cat "$LOCK" 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^[0-9]+$" || echo 1); NEW=$((DEPTH-1)); if [ $NEW -le 0 ]; then rm -f "$LOCK"; else printf "%s" "$NEW" > "$LOCK"; fi) 200>.claude/agent-depth.lock.flock; exit 0'

bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); TITLE=$(jq -r ".task.title // .title // unknown" <<< "$INPUT" 2>/dev/null); mkdir -p .claude 2>/dev/null || true; printf "[%s] TASK done: %s\n" "$(date -u +%T)" "$TITLE" >> .claude/session-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>/dev/null; jq -n "{systemMessage: \"coder-workflow notice: Task complete. MANDATORY: Did you over-engineer the solution? Zero dummy code allowed! (Note: Changes are being auto-committed to git in the background).\"}"; git add . 2>/dev/null; nohup bash -c "if git diff --cached --quiet; then exit 0; fi; MSG=\$(git diff --cached | head -n 500 | claude -p \"Write a 1-line conventional commit message summarizing these staged changes. Output ONLY the raw commit message, no markdown.\" --setting-sources user --no-session-persistence 2>/dev/null | tail -n 1); if printf \"%s\" \"\$MSG\" | grep -qiE \"no staged changes|staging area is empty|already committed\"; then MSG=\"chore: auto-commit task - $TITLE\"; elif [ -z \"\$MSG\" ]; then MSG=\"chore: auto-commit task - $TITLE\"; fi; git commit -m \"\$MSG\" 2>/dev/null || true" >/dev/null 2>&1 & exit 0; command -v coder-workflow >/dev/null 2>&1 && coder-workflow experience record --type task-done --outcome success 2>/dev/null || true; exit 0'
bash

jq -n '{systemMessage: "coder-workflow notice: Session is ending. If you solved a complex bug or made a major architectural decision, run \"/memory store <lesson>\" before closing to save it to your Long-Term Memory Bank."}'
